alpha_insight: Haize Labs occupies a unique position: they are hired by the very labs (Anthropic, etc.) building frontier models to break those models. The insight is that safety is a scaling problem — you can't manually red-team models fast enough, so you need ML systems to adversarially attack other ML systems. This creates a durable moat as models get more capable.

name: Kevin Wang
affiliation: OpenAI (Researcher), Princeton University
problems_worked_on: Scaling depth in reinforcement learning (1000-layer networks)
Goal-conditioned RL without demonstrations or rewards
what_makes_them_spiky: NeurIPS 2025 Best Paper Award — '1000 Layer Networks for Self-Supervised RL'
Demonstrated 2x-50x performance improvements by scaling RL networks to 1024 layers
Went from undergraduate research seminar at Princeton to winning NeurIPS Best Paper
Now Researcher at OpenAI
Challenged decades of conventional wisdom that deep networks don't work in RL
key_work: 1000 Layer Networks for Self-Supervised RL: Scaling Depth Can Enable New Goal-Reaching Capabilities (NeurIPS 2025 Best Paper)
Proved RL is ready for scaling breakthroughs analogous to language and vision
2x-50x performance improvements in unsupervised goal-conditioned settings
current_work: Researcher at OpenAI; Princeton undergraduate who won NeurIPS Best Paper and is now working at the frontier of AI
alpha_insight: Kevin's NeurIPS Best Paper proved the most contrarian result in recent RL: that depth scaling (up to 1024 layers) unlocks massive gains in self-supervised RL, while the entire field has been using 2-5 layer networks for decades. This is the 'scaling hypothesis' hitting RL — the same insight that transformed NLP and vision. At OpenAI, he's presumably applying this scaling intuition to their RL post-training pipelines, which is the secret sauce behind o1/o3.
